title: Engine observability — diagnostics, logging, motion + renderer probes
summary: Cross-cutting engine work that makes runtime + render-side bugs diagnosable in one curl. Spans logging policy + CI gate, structured lifecycle traces, named per-system debug loggers, doctor diagnostics section, boot noise budget, entity-recreated detection, the first-class renderer/scene probe, the manualChunks investigation for the WebGPU lazy split, and a motion-smoothness probe with targeted fixes for tween / lane-assist micro-stutters.
doneCriteria: console.log is banned in engine/** and examples/**/src/** (CI gate)
every engine system that emits diagnostics uses a named debug logger gated by AGF_DEBUG
engine doctor surfaces a diagnostics + renderer-probe section
Vite manualChunks actually splits three.webgpu — main `three-*` chunk budget back ≤ 400 KB
motion-smoothness e2e probe asserts a frame-delta P95 threshold + ships a fix when it regresses
